risk_if_not_implemented: Without Post-Sensitive / Regulated Data Spill Operations, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Incident Response Plan (IRP)
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to maintain and make available a current and viable Incident Response Plan (IRP) to all stakeholders.
compensating_control_1.justification: Incident Response Plan (IRP) (IRO-04) provides incident response capability that compensates for the absence of Post-Sensitive / Regulated Data Spill Operations (IRO-12.3) by enabling timely detection, containment, and recovery from security events in the absence of the primary control. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Sensitive / Regulated Data Spill Response
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to respond to sensitive/regulated data spills.
compensating_control_2.justification: Sensitive / Regulated Data Spill Response (IRO-12) provides incident response capability that compensates for the absence of Post-Sensitive / Regulated Data Spill Operations (IRO-12.3) by enabling timely detection, containment, and recovery from security events in the absence of the primary control. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
